title: Bobotie with almonds – all in one dish

seo.title: Bobotie with almonds - all in one dish - Marinda Kook

html:
I always used to fry my mince beforehand with my onions and herbs. Here at this year's KKNK I heard Herman say that he puts his meat in raw. I was a bit hesitant to do it that way, but decided I'd try. I must say it's juicier. The meat builds up its own little sauce and binds wonderfully with the bread and the egg. From now on this is how I make my bobotie.

 

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 25 ml oil
  • 1 onion
  • 750g – 1kg good mince
  • 3 slices of bread
  • 125 ml milk
  • 1 egg beaten
  • 80 ml apricot jam
  • 60 ml vinegar
  • 60 ml medium curry powder
  • 30 ml turmeric
  • 1 apple grated
  • 2 lemon leaves or bay leaves
  • salt and pepper to taste
  • flaked almonds to sprinkle on top

TOPPING

  • 1 egg lightly beaten in a cup with 125 ml milk

NOW:

  1. fry your chopped onion in the oil until glassy
  2. let the bread soak in the milk and crumble it into your meat in your mixing bowl
  3. mix all the rest of the ingredients into a mixing bowl except the topping ingredients, almonds and lemon leaves
  4. now mix the cooked onions into the meat
  5. spoon the meat into a greased oven dish – don't pack it in tightly
  6. press the lemon leaves halfway into the meat mixture
  7. sprinkle the almonds over
  8. pour the topping over ( egg and milk lightly beaten)
  9. bake for one hour at 180 degrees celsius

Enjoy your bobotie with yellow rice and raisins and chutney. Steamed dried fruit is also a winner as a side dish. It remains a traditional dish that never goes out of fashion.

Tomorrow the bobotie tastes even better.
